title: Fabrication of Microneedle Patches
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ddou4","text":"The goal is to create a microneedle. patch, which, by applying it on a leaf, will extract amplification-ready DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ku36","text":"All MN patches used for DNA extraction were fabricated using  polydimethylsiloxane molds. These molds were fabricated by laser  ablation, and the dimension of each mold is approximately 10 mm × 10 mm,  which has 15 × 15 arrays of microneedle conical cavities. The height of  each cavity is 800 μm, and the diameters of the tip and base are 10 and  300 μm, respectively.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Heat deionised water to 100 degrees Celsius 100 °C using a becher.
Put the mold(s) into the water and stir softly.
Remove the molds and let them dry in a chemical hood for 1-2 hours.
Stick the molds on the bottom of an adequate recipient using double-sided tape.
Add 4g of poly(vinyl alcohol) (30− 70 kDa, 10 wt %) powder and stir until transparent.
Heat 28g of deionised water to 110 degrees Celsius 110 °C .
Add 1.5 mL of PVA solution on top of the mold.
Centrifuge at 4000 rmp for 20 minutes at 40 degrees Celsius.
Let the microneedles dry under a chemical hood overnight.
Remove the microneedles from the molds.
The microneedles are ready for use.
creator: Dana Mozaffari
